none.justification: Correctly identifies plain SET as not type-checking, gives both SET-specific gated paths (SET GET via getGenericCommand/checkType and IFEQ/IFNE/IFDEQ/IFDNE direct checkType), and names object.c:887 addReplyErrorObject(c, shared.wrongtypeerr) as the reply write site. Minor inclusion of SETNX/SETEX/PSETEX in the broad shared-function chain is imprecise, but the SET-specific answer is complete.

ccodegraph.justification: Fully matches the GT: it distinguishes the shared wrongtype object from the actual client reply write in checkType at object.c:887, traces both SET-only option paths through setGenericCommand, and notes that plain SET overwrites without WRONGTYPE.

lsp.justification: Fully correct: it states plain SET does not WRONGTYPE, identifies both valid SET option-gated branches, and lands on checkType/object.c:887 as the actual construction/send point for the WRONGTYPE reply.
